title: How to explain whacky stuff to normal people

transcript.text: Hello. This video is my thoughts on how to teach people, how to explain stuff to people, especially stuff like new age stuff that's kind of out of the realm of the zeitgeist, you know, kind of, I guess you could say controversial stuff, new information type stuff, you know, or if you're talking to somebody who's like closed minded stuff.

I did a video the other day about this, but I wanted to do another one to explain some stuff that I didn't explain there. And, um, I think it's really important when you explaining stuff to people, especially if it's something that's new to them, you know, and you're like teaching them type of a situation, not to, uh, not to put your ego into it, like not to give them the impression that you're smarter than them or, you know, more than them stuff or stuff.

Cause you know, a lot of people will be, um, kind of, um, um, um, turned off by that and not want to hear you. And also if you, uh, are you too, if you're like too instructional, um, it can, you can cause stress in the conversation.

Cause you know what people like to do when they hear people say stuff is they like to talk to about their own opinions about things. And you know, if you get really excited, you can kind of be like, yeah, to kind of try to keep them in line and be like, yeah, but don't talk about that. I'm trying to talk, tell you about this.

So basically what I do is like, if I try to explain something to somebody, like even if it's something simple, like a scientific concept, like redshift or something, um, you know, and then the person interrupts me and then they go and they talk about their idea of it, even though it has nothing to do with what we're talking about.

You know, they like stray from the conversation and start talking about something else. I don't try to bring them back to the original conversation because what I did is I sparked some idea in their mind and that's, that's good. That's what you want to do in the first place.

So I just, I just, I just let them take the conversation in the direction that they want to go. And I let them get totally sidetracked and we both get sidetracked and we talk about something else or whatever they want to talk about.

And, uh, you know, and then I, I ask them questions, as many questions as I can about it. And I make them the teacher for me and, uh, you know, and then I take them down the path of explaining to me as much as they want, as much as they can.

And then when it peters out, I go right back to the original thing that I tried to explain them about, you know, and then I start, I start from the very beginning and then I, uh, you know, and then I go until they, uh, change the subject.
And then, so I ask them questions and we go down their little path, their little road or whatever they want to talk about, you know, cause in the end it's good.
It makes me feel good because I know that I sparked an intelligent conversation with somebody and I'm, you know, we're not just talking about the weather or some superficial thing.
Like, I hate those kinds of conversations. I can't stand those kinds of conversations, uh, because, you know, everybody has those kinds of conversations.
I mean, I like those kinds of conversations, but not if I'm like, but, but I much prefer to have like a real conversation, you know, and like one person is explaining something to the other person.
So anyways, and then that peters out and then I, um, and then I go back to the original idea and then I start over again.

And, you know, now sometimes, you know, if you get, because a lot of what people are doing is if you're explaining like, you know, new age concepts, complicated concepts that are out of people's, um, realm of, um, familiarity, people will, even if it's subconsciously, deliberately avoid it because it, because the unknown makes people, um, uncomfortable and scared, you know?
So they'll do whatever they want to, they can to avoid it, you know, but the idea is to let them avoid it because if you try to bring them back, then, you know, it can cause stress.

And then it'll, and if there's any stress in a conversation, then the person will just be like, well, we'll, we'll rebel on them and then you won't be, and then you won't be able to even continue the conversation at all.
